focus_areas.title: The meaning and grammar of Rev 14:12 — hode he hupomone ton hagion estin
The hode (here is) formula as a structural device in Revelation 13-14
The Romans 5:3-5 endurance production chain (thlipsis → hupomone → dokime → elpis)
The hupomone/makrothumia distinction — patience with circumstances vs. patience with persons
James 5:7-11 — Job and the prophets as endurance models
Hebrews 10:36-39 — patience needed to receive the promise
Revelation 12:11 — the three-fold mechanism of overcoming/endurance
Active endurance vs. passive resignation — the character of hupomone
The hupomone thread through Revelation — from John's introduction to the patience declaration
The nikao-hupomone relationship — endurance as sustained posture, overcoming as achieved outcome
OT endurance theology — waiting, hoping, trusting in the LORD
The eschatological context — endurance under the beast crisis (Rev 13-14)
